title: YCB Content Layer Integration
description: Integrate Your Commonbase architectural patterns into N5 OS: Entry/Comment threading, Content Carts, Preprocessor Pipeline, and Semantic Links

D1.1.name: Database Schema Migration
D1.1.description: Create content_threads, thread_comments, and entry_links tables

D2.1.name: CLI Thread Commands
D2.1.description: Implement n5 content comment, n5 content thread commands

D2.2.name: CLI Cart Commands
D2.2.description: Implement n5 content cart create, add, synthesize commands

D3.1.name: Preprocessor Pipeline
D3.1.description: Create preprocessor.py with trafilatura, extraction, cleaning stages

D4.1.name: Semantic Links Engine
D4.1.description: Implement auto-discovery of entry relationships via embeddings

lineage.moment: V asked to implement YCB architectural patterns using Pulse

summary:
**D1.1:** Successfully implemented YCB-style Entry/Comment threading schema for the N5 Content Library. Created migration script with 3 core tables (content_threads, thread_comments, entry_links) and comprehensive Python helper functions. All functionality tested with real data from the content library, including nested comment hierarchies and entry linking.
**D1.2:** Successfully created Content Carts schema with content_carts and cart_items tables, including migration script and comprehensive Python helper functions. All functionality tested and verified.
**D2.1:** Successfully implemented all 6 CLI thread commands for the YCB Content Layer. Created comprehensive CLI at N5/scripts/n5_content_cli.py with proper argument parsing, error handling, and tree visualization. All commands tested end-to-end with real data including nested comment hierarchies.
**D2.2:** Successfully implemented all 9 CLI cart commands with full functionality including synthesis via /zo/ask API. Extended existing n5_content_cli.py with comprehensive cart management capabilities.
**D3.1:** Successfully implemented the complete ContentPreprocessor pipeline for YCB-style content ingestion. Created a 4-stage pipeline (Extract → Clean → Enrich → Store) with comprehensive entity extraction, content classification, and metadata enrichment. All CLI integrations are working, and the existing content_ingest.py has been refactored to use the new pipeline. Pipeline handles HTML (trafilatura), PDF (pdfplumber), and Markdown files with robust error handling and dry-run support.
**D4.1:** Successfully implemented the Semantic Links Engine for YCB Content Layer. Created SemanticLinkEngine class that auto-discovers relationships between content entries using embeddings and similarity scoring. Engine leverages existing N5 vector store infrastructure (vectors_v2.db) and computes cosine similarity between content items. Links are classified by strength (very_similar, related, loosely_related) and stored in the entry_links table. All CLI commands are functional and integrated.
